armed-conflicts.headline: Ukraine hit by Russia; Greenland dispute sparks trade war
armed-conflicts.summary: Russia launched over 300 drones and missiles at Ukraine's power grid, causing heating outages in Kyiv amidst freezing temperatures as Ukrainian officials engaged in US and UAE-led peace talks. Geopolitical tensions escalated due to President Trump's renewed interest in acquiring Greenland, triggering threats of US tariffs on European nations and concerns for the NATO alliance.

regions.name: Kyiv, Ukraine
regions.detail: Russia launched drones and missiles, causing heating outages amidst freezing temperatures.

regions.name: Greenland
regions.detail: President Trump's renewed interest in acquiring it escalated tensions, sparking trade war threats.

regions.detail: Engaged in peace talks for Ukraine and saw Trump's renewed interest in acquiring Greenland.

regions.detail: Ukrainian officials engaged in US and UAE-led peace talks at this location.

regions.detail: Engaged in leading US and UAE-led peace talks for Ukraine.

human-rights.headline: Iran protests crackdown, US rights erosion, DR Congo civilian risks
human-rights.summary: A report from the International Centre for Human Rights, released on January 20, 2026, alleged that at least 43,000 people have been killed in Iran's protest crackdown, leading to a UN Human Rights Council special session. Amnesty International criticized the Trump administration for eroding human rights in the United States through authoritarian practices. In the Democratic Republic of Congo, the M23 withdrawal from Uvira put civilians at grave risk from abusive militias.

regions.name: Tehran, Iran
regions.detail: A report alleged 43,000 killed in protest crackdowns, prompting a UN Human Rights Council session.

regions.name: Washington D.C., USA
regions.detail: Amnesty International criticized the Trump administration for eroding human rights via authoritarian practices.

regions.name: Uvira, DR Congo
regions.detail: The M23 withdrawal put civilians at grave risk from abusive militias.

regions.name: Geneva, Switzerland
regions.detail: A UN Human Rights Council special session was prompted by alleged killings in Iran.

climate-events.headline: Global water bankruptcy, uncounted emissions, US exits Paris accord
climate-events.summary: A UN report on January 20, 2026, declared the dawn of an 'era of global water bankruptcy' due to chronic depletion and pollution. A study highlighted growing uncounted greenhouse gas emissions from natural ecosystems, potentially shortening global climate action timelines. The United States formally exited the Paris Agreement on Climate Change, UNFCCC, and IPCC on this date. Additionally, parts of the US were affected by drought, and Mozambique experienced severe floods.

regions.name: Global
regions.detail: A UN report declared an 'era of global water bankruptcy'; uncounted emissions were highlighted.

regions.detail: Experienced severe floods that affected over 513,000 people.

regions.detail: Natural ecosystems in the region were subject to a study on uncounted greenhouse gas emissions.

regions.detail: The region was affected by drought as part of broader US climate impacts.

public-health.headline: Mpox, Nipah, yellow fever outbreaks; screwworm alert
public-health.summary: Madagascar is racing to contain a growing mpox outbreak with confirmed cases including children, exacerbating a multi-layered humanitarian crisis. Bangladesh reported a confirmed Nipah virus case linked to raw date palm sap consumption. Colombia reactivated a yellow fever alert following 10 human cases and deaths, alongside widespread non-human primate deaths. The CDC also issued an advisory for a New World Screwworm outbreak moving into Northern Mexico.

regions.name: Antananarivo, Madagascar
regions.detail: The capital faced efforts to contain a growing mpox outbreak, including among children.

regions.detail: The CDC issued an advisory for a New World Screwworm outbreak moving into the region.

economic-crises.headline: US-EU tariff threats hit markets; IMF forecasts resilient growth
economic-crises.summary: US tariff threats against European nations over Greenland caused a significant selloff in Asian and US stock markets, with the S&P 500 falling 2.1%. Despite escalating trade tensions, the IMF projected resilient global growth of 3.3% for 2026, largely offsetting trade disruption with a surge in AI investment, but flagged ongoing trade risks. Edelman's 2026 Trust Barometer indicated that national insularity threatens the global economy.

regions.name: United States
regions.detail: Tariff threats against European nations caused a significant stock market selloff; IMF projected resilient growth.

regions.name: Europe
regions.detail: Faced US tariff threats over Greenland, contributing to escalating trade tensions.

regions.detail: Stock markets experienced a significant selloff due to US tariff threats against European nations.

regions.detail: IMF projected resilient global growth despite trade tensions, with national insularity threatening the economy.

democratic-erosion.headline: Amnesty reports US democratic erosion under Trump administration
democratic-erosion.summary: Amnesty International released a report on January 20, 2026, detailing a 'dangerous trajectory' of increasing authoritarian practices and erosion of human rights in the United States under the Trump administration. The report highlighted attacks on freedom of the press, expression, civil society organizations, and the legal system, undermining democratic norms.

regions.name: Washington D.C., USA
regions.detail: Amnesty International reported increasing authoritarian practices and erosion of democratic norms under the administration.

humanitarian.headline: Gaza crisis dire, Sudan famine, Madagascar floods, aid cuts hit children
humanitarian.summary: The humanitarian crisis in Gaza remains dire due to winter conditions and lack of aid, with Egypt sending its 120th aid convoy. Famine conditions persist in Sudan amidst sharply fallen humanitarian funding. Floods in Mozambique affected over 513,000 people, threatening children with unsafe water and malnutrition, while education for children in Thailand-Myanmar border camps reached a 'breaking point' due to aid cuts. MSF also reported that US policy changes have severely damaged global health and humanitarian programs.
